title: tripterygium wilfordii var. regelii
intro: Tripterygium wilfordii var. regelii, also known as Leech Bitters or Thunder of God Vine, is a plant species native to China and parts of Asia. It is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and immunosuppressive effects, which make it a popular ingredient in traditional Chinese medicine. The plant has been used to treat various health conditions, including rheumatoid arthritis, lupus, and other autoimmune diseases, as well as skin conditions such as eczema and psoriasis. Herbal preparations of Tripterygium wilfordii var. regelii can be found in various forms, including capsules, tablets, and tinctures, often in combination with other herbs to enhance its therapeutic effects.
